page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.c1 family. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning any storage requirements must be fulfilled via external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.c1.large is designed for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. This profile is suitable for batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics where compute density is prioritized over memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance within the ecs.c1 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, providing 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for data persistence. Lacking hyperthreading, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable execution times for compute-bound tasks. The ecs.c1.large is structured for workloads prioritizing raw processing power over memory capacity, making it suitable for web serving, batch processing, and moderate computational tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, running on the KVM hypervisor for predictable compute workloads.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring eight physical cores and a balanced two gigabytes of memory per core for predictable processing.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core
16.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No local storage or integrated GPUs
Suitable for compute-intensive workloads and batch processing
